name: Mitochondrial transfusion increased hippocampal SDHB in aged mice
summary: A second aged-mouse study supports target-tissue mitochondrial biomarker movement after young mitochondrial transfusion.
statement: In aged mice, intravenous transfusion of mitochondria isolated from young mouse liver significantly improved a hippocampal mitochondrial-function marker by up-regulating mitochondrial complex II protein subunit SDHB.
population_or_model: Aged C57BL/6 mice compared with young mice and young-mitochondria-treated aged mice
quantitative_note: The abstract reports a significant decrease in mitochondrial complex proteins I-V in aged brain tissue and significant SDHB up-regulation after mitochondrial transfusion.
caveats: This is a tissue biomarker study rather than a functional aging-outcome study.
The age model and short follow-up do not establish durable healthspan benefit.
Mechanism, biodistribution, and immune handling after systemic mitochondrial delivery remain unresolved.
